title: Sony WH-1000XM6 vs. WH-1000XM5: which should you buy?
author: Electriz TechTalk
excerpt: If you're looking for a new pair of noise-cancelling cans, then chances are Sony's WH-1000XM6 and XM5 have already made your shortlist. But which are...
content:
If you're looking for a new pair of noise-cancelling cans, then chances are Sony's WH-1000XM6 and XM5 have already made your shortlist. But which are the best Sony headphones of 2025?

The newer XM6, launched in May this year, might feel like an obvious pick. But their recent release has made the older Sony WH-1000XM5 over-ears much more affordable. We've compared them to help you decide which model is the better fit...

## Sony WH-1000XM6 vs. WH-1000XM5: specs compared

| Specifications | WH-1000XM6 | WH-1000XM5 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Active Noise Cancellation (ANC) | Yes | Yes |
| Drivers | 30mm dynamic | 30mm dynamic |
| Spatial Audio | Available on all platforms | Selected platforms only |
| Microphones | 12 (with AI-based beamforming) | 8 |
| Connectivity | Bluetooth 5.3 | Bluetooth 5.2 |
| Battery Life | 30 hours (with ANC on) | 30 hours (with ANC on) |
| Foldable Design | Yes | No |
| Weight | 254g | 250g |

## Price and availability

Sony's WH-1000XM6 headphones hit the shelves in May this year at £399. The WH-1000XM5 can now be snapped up for just £279 -- a lot less than what they cost at launch in 2022.

## Design and comfort

At first glance, you'd struggle to tell these noise-cancelling headphones apart. But the Sony WH-1000XM6 does have a few subtle yet nifty design upgrades. The biggest difference is the foldable design that returns from the XM4 era, with stainless-steel hinges that let the earcups swivel and collapse inward. The XM6 also has a wider headband and improved mic mesh for windy conditions.

## Sound quality

Sony 1000X Series headphones have always delivered great audio quality. The XM6 brings upgraded drivers with perforations in their voice coil and a stiffer dome housing, resulting in richer vocals and increased clarity. A wider soundstage and improved D/A conversion via the new QN3 processor round out the improvements.

## Noise cancellation

The XM6's QN3 processor is seven times faster than the XM5's chip and fine-tunes 12 mics (four more than the XM5) in real time. An Adaptive NC Optimiser constantly adjusts noise cancellation based on your environment and how the headphones fit on your head.

## Other features

The XM6 uses AI-based beamforming for crystal-clear calls in windy conditions. It also features 360 Reality Audio Upmix for Cinema, adding spatial audio processing to stereo films and shows.

## Battery life and charging

Both models provide up to 30 hours with ANC on. New to the XM6 is the ability to top up the battery while streaming over Bluetooth -- so no downtime during recharges.

## Should you get the Sony WH-1000XM6 or WH-1000XM5?

The XM6 has clear advantages in noise-cancelling, sound precision, and foldable design. But if you're on a tighter budget, the XM5 remains a solid choice.

## FAQs

### Can I use the Sony WH-1000XM6 with iPhone or Android?

Yes, they work with both via Bluetooth. The Sony Sound Connect app is available on iOS and Android.

### Can you use Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones for gaming?

Yes -- they feature Game EQ for first-person shooters and support low-latency Bluetooth LE Audio.

### What's included in the box?

Carrying case, connection cable, USB cable, reference guide, and warranty card.

### Should I upgrade from the WH-1000XM4 to the WH-1000XM6?

If you've got the budget, you'll notice a big improvement in sound detail, noise cancellation, and call quality.
